incidents.victim: Government agencies, critical infrastructure worldwide (200+ organizations)
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in SOHO routers (MikroTik, TP-Link) to hijack DNS settings and intercept OAuth tokens from Microsoft Office users without deploying malware.
incidents.impact: Over 18,000 routers across 120+ countries compromised, enabling credential theft from 200+ organizations including foreign ministries and law enforcement. DOJ launched Operation Masquerade to reset affected US routers.

incidents.victim: U.S. energy, water, and government facilities
incidents.vector: Exploited internet-exposed Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley programmable logic controllers (PLCs) connected via cellular networks.
incidents.impact: Nearly 3,900 exposed PLCs in the United States targeted across energy, water, and government sectors. Some victims experienced financial losses. Attacks escalated since the start of the U.S.-Iran conflict in March 2026.

incidents.vector: Researcher 'Chaotic Eclipse' released proof-of-concept exploit for an unpatched Windows zero-day (BlueHammer) combining a TOCTOU race condition and path confusion in Windows Defender's signature update system.
incidents.impact: A local attacker can access the Security Account Manager (SAM) database, obtain password hashes, and escalate to administrator rights. Microsoft has not yet patched the flaw. Ransomware gangs expected to weaponize within days.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, and financial organizations (U.S., UK, Australia)
incidents.vector: Exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in SmarterMail and GoAnywhere MFT, sometimes one week before public disclosure, to breach web-facing systems.
incidents.impact: Medusa ransomware operators moved from initial access to data exfiltration and ransomware deployment within 24 hours. Victims include University of Mississippi Medical Center and Passaic County, NJ. UMMC fully reopened March 2 with FBI/DHS help.

incidents.vector: Phishing attack by the Silent Ransom Group targeted an attorney leading Federal Circuit cases involving intellectual property.
incidents.impact: Hackers accessed files for 10 clients of the 2,400-lawyer firm, which previously represented President Trump. Stolen materials were posted on the group's website on March 30.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-35616 (severity 9.1/10) in FortiClient EMS, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via crafted requests.
incidents.impact: Active in-the-wild exploitation began on March 31, timed around the Easter holiday weekend. CISA ordered federal agencies to patch immediately. This is the second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks.

incidents.vector: German Federal Criminal Police (BKA) identified 31-year-old Russian Daniil Shchukin as 'UNKN,' the leader of both GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations.
incidents.impact: BKA attributed 130+ acts of computer sabotage and extortion in Germany between 2019-2021 to Shchukin and an associate, causing €35 million in economic damage. Shchukin is believed to reside in Krasnodar, Russia.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed a wiper payload (CanisterWorm) that checks victim timezone and locale for Iran, then destroys data on Kubernetes clusters or local machines.
incidents.impact: If the target is in Iran and has Kubernetes access, the wiper destroys data on every node. Otherwise it wipes the local machine. The campaign leveraged the same infrastructure used in TeamPCP's earlier supply chain attacks.

incidents.vector: Exploited CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious payloads via trusted update channels, deploying Havoc and ShadowPad malware.
incidents.impact: A government-operated TrueConf server serving dozens of government entities was compromised, pushing weaponized updates to all connected endpoints. CISA ordered federal agencies to patch by April 16.

incidents.vector: Hackers infiltrated the museum's network, took control of the photographic server, and sent a ransom demand to the Uffizi director's personal phone.
incidents.impact: The attack on February 1 disrupted operations while backups were restored. The Uffizi denied that security maps were stolen or that any data was lost, but accelerated a camera upgrade program.

incidents.vector: ASIGINT, a subsidiary of Italian firm SIO, tricked approximately 200 users into installing a fake version of WhatsApp rigged with spyware.
incidents.impact: This is the second time in 15 months that Meta disrupted spyware activity in Italy. The campaign was 'highly targeted' and relied on social engineering to deliver the malicious app.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-3055 (severity 9.3/10) in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway, enabling unauthenticated disclosure of sensitive memory contents.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered emergency patching. The vulnerability has hallmarks of the original CitrixBleed and CitrixBleed Two, both of which caused widespread enterprise compromises. Previously used against the Pennsylvania Attorney General and Netherlands Public Prosecution Service.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2025-53521 — an unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager.
incidents.impact: F5 confirmed active exploitation. NCSC and CISA urged all organizations using BIG-IP APM to take immediate action, investigate for compromise, and rebuild affected systems. Common in large enterprises.

incidents.vector: LAPSUS$ released a 3 GB data archive for free after failing to sell it, containing GitHub user information, employee data, and internal source code structures.
incidents.impact: Partially verified dump includes data from clinical research subsidiaries and internal developer information. AstraZeneca has not confirmed or denied the breach after 96+ hours, creating potential GDPR and HIPAA exposure.

incidents.vector: Attackers breached the European Commission's Amazon Web Services (AWS) account on March 24.
incidents.impact: Internal data may have been stolen from the EU's executive arm before the breach was detected and blocked. An internal investigation is ongoing to establish the full extent. This follows a prior incident that exposed staff details.

incidents.vector: Iranian cyberattack disrupted Stryker's operations amid the broader Iranian campaign against U.S. targets.
incidents.impact: The medtech giant experienced operational disruption but reported it was back up. The attack occurred as Iranian state-backed groups escalated targeting of U.S. companies during the U.S.-Iran conflict.

incidents.victim: Software supply chain (Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, LiteLLM, VS Code plug-ins)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP compromised GitHub Actions for Trivy and Checkmarx KICS, poisoned LiteLLM PyPI packages, and published malicious VS Code extensions to steal credentials from CI/CD pipelines.
incidents.impact: Credential-stealing malware extracted SSH keys, cloud credentials, API tokens, and Docker configurations. LiteLLM is present in 36% of cloud environments. TeamPCP now runs dual ransomware tracks (CipherForce and Vect). CISA issued a KEV with April 8 deadline.

incidents.vector: North Korean actors posed as a fake company, socially engineered the lead Axios NPM developer via a video call, and tricked him into installing malware that gave access to publish compromised package versions.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions of the widely-used Axios HTTP client library were published to NPM, though detected and removed within hours. The attack is separate from the TeamPCP campaign despite timing overlap.

incidents.vector: North Korean operatives posed as a quantitative trading firm at a major crypto conference, deposited $1M of their own funds to build trust, then exploited access to steal from Drift Ecosystem Vaults.
incidents.impact: $285 million in cryptocurrency stolen from the decentralized exchange. The attackers spent months building credibility with Drift staff between December 2025 and January 2026.

incidents.vector: Iranian hacking group Handala claimed to have compromised FBI Director Patel's personal email and data.
incidents.impact: The FBI confirmed the theft of the director's personal emails. The State Department reissued a $10 million reward for information on the Iranian hackers involved.

incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised routers and web cameras, launching record-setting DDoS attacks against targets including U.S. Department of Defense networks.
incidents.impact: DOJ, with Canadian and German authorities, dismantled the infrastructure. The botnets launched hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks and demanded extortion payments from victims.

incidents.vector: Social engineering attack targeting the company's customer support system.
incidents.impact: Stolen data primarily included customer names and email addresses. The telehealth giant confirmed the breach and notified affected customers.
